relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Elihu’s heart trembles before the storm.
He calls Job to hear the roar of the divine voice.
Lightning runs to the corners of the earth.
Thunder follows with majestic voice.
The Divine thunders wonder beyond human knowing.
Snow and rain answer command.
Human hands are sealed so people may know the work beyond them.
Animals retreat to dens.
Storm and cold come from hidden chambers.
The breath of the Divine gives ice and constrains waters.
Clouds are loaded and lightning scattered.
The storm turns by divine guidance over the inhabited earth.
It may come as correction, land-care, or covenant-kindness.
Elihu tells Job to stand still and consider wonder.
Can Job know how cloud-light is commanded?
Can Job know the balance of clouds?
Can he explain heat and stillness under the south wind?
Can he spread the skies like a cast mirror?
Humans cannot arrange speech before such darkness.
Who can announce that they will speak before such majesty?
Light may be hidden until wind clears the sky.
Brightness comes from the north, and majesty surrounds the Divine.
Shaddai cannot be searched out; power, justice, and righteousness surround him.
Therefore humans stand in awe, and self-wise hearts are not the measure.
